title: The Bangladesh Petroleum Act, 1974
description: An Act to provide for the exploration, development, exploitation, production, processing, refining and marketing of petroleum. WHEREAS it is expedient to provide for the exploration, development, exploitation, production, processing, refining and marketing of petroleum; It is hereby enacted as follows:-

provisions.title: 1. Short title, extent and commencement
provisions.content:
1. (1) This Act may be called the Bangladesh Petroleum Act, 1974 .
(2) It extends to the whole of Bangladesh and shall also apply to the economic zone and continental shelf of Bangladesh.
(3) It shall be deemed to have come into force on the 22nd day of August, 1974.

provisions.title: 2. Definitions
3. Right of Government to explore, etc. of Petroleum
4. Petroleum agreement
provisions.content:
4. (1) The Government may enter into a petroleum agreement with any person for the purpose of any petroleum operation.
(2) No person shall undertake or carry on any petroleum operation except under a petroleum agreement entered into under sub-section (1):
Provided that the Government may, within one month from the date of commencement of this Act, permit, on such conditions as it deems fit, any person to carry on any petroleum operation otherwise than under a petroleum agreement for a period not exceeding six months.

provisions.title: 5. Power of inspection
6. Duties of persons engaged in petroleum operation
7. Land required for petroleum operation to be deemed for public purpose
provisions.content: 7. Any land required for carrying on any petroleum operation shall be deemed to be required for a public purpose.

provisions.title: 8. Delegation of powers
provisions.content: [8. The Government may, by order in writing, delegate to the Bangladesh Oil and Gas Corporation, that is, Petrobangla, or to any other Government agency, subject to such conditions, if any, as may be specified therein, such of its rights and powers under this Act, or the rules made thereunder as may be so specified.]

provisions.title: 9. Penalty
provisions.content:
9. (1) Whoever contravenes any provision of this Act or the rules made thereunder shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
(2) Where an offence punishable under sub-section (1) is committed by a firm, company or other body corporate, every partner, director, manager, secretary or other officer or agent thereof shall, if actively concerned in the conduct of the business of such firm, company or body corporate, be deemed to have committed the offence unless he proves that the offence was committed without his knowledge or that he exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of the offence.

provisions.title: 10. Indemnity
provisions.content: 10. No suit, prosecution or other legal proceeding shall lie against any person for anything which is in good faith done or intended to be done under this Act or the rules made thereunder.

provisions.title: 11. Power to make rules
provisions.content: 11. The Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, make rules for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

provisions.title: 12. Repeals
provisions.content: 12. The Regulation of Mines, Oil-fields and Mineral Development (Government Control) Act, 1948 (XXIV of 1948), and the Petroleum Ordinance, 1974 (Ord. XVI of 1974), are hereby repealed.

definitions.definition: a production sharing agreement or any other agreement or contract relating to any petroleum operation
any activity related to exploration, development, exploitation, production, processing, refining or marketing of petroleum.
